data.title: World Street | Kospi sinks 2%, Microsoft's faces $1.3 bn lawsuit, UK inflation spikes and more
data.description:
World Street offers a sneak peek into the world of business and economy.

South Korea's benchmark Kospi index dragged 2 percent amid political upheaval in the country.  Microsoft has been charged with a $1.3 billion lawsuit alleging unfairly overcharging customers of rival cloud companies. UK inflation picked up sharply to a higher-than-expected 2.3 percent in October, squashing hopes of a December interest rate cut from the Bank of England. All this and more in the December 4 edition of World Street.

Total chaos

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

South Korea’s markets opened sharply lower Wednesday following a day of political chaos, where President Yoon Suk Yeol imposed and later rescinded a martial law decree.

The Kospi fell 1.8 percent, and the Kosdaq dropped 2.1 percent, slightly paring losses after reports that the financial regulator could deploy a 10 trillion won ($7.07 billion) stabilisation fund to support markets, according to Yonhap News Agency.

Heavy penalty

Microsoft has been hit with a £1 billion ($1.27 billion) lawsuit alleging it unfairly overcharged customers using rival cloud platforms, including Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Alibaba Cloud.

The lawsuit claims Microsoft’s pricing structure forces higher costs on customers licensing its Windows Server software on competitors' infrastructure while offering discounts for its Azure cloud users. Plaintiffs argue this creates an unfair disadvantage, effectively penalizing firms opting for alternative cloud solutions.

Getting out of hand

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Inflation in the UK surged to 2.3 percent in October, up from 1.7 percent in September and surpassing the 2.2 percent forecast, according to the Office for National Statistics. The rise pushes inflation above the Bank of England's 2 percent target, likely curbing chances of a December rate cut.

Pressure mounts

Intel stock tumbled 6 percent overnight, marking its worst day since September, following the abrupt exit of CEO Pat Gelsinger. Gelsinger's four-year tenure saw Intel lose market share and falter in artificial intelligence.

The company announced CFO David Zinsner and products chief MJ Holthaus as interim co-CEOs, with board member Frank Yeary stepping in as interim executive chair. Intel's stock is down over 50 percent this year as the search for a permanent CEO begins.

Shopping spree

Nearly 197 million Americans shopped during Thanksgiving through Cyber Monday, surpassing the National Retail Federation’s forecast of 183.4 million but falling short of 2023's 200 million.

Shoppers spent an average of $235, $8 more than last year, with top purchases including apparel, toys, and personal care items. The data highlights retailers’ success in attracting deal-hungry consumers despite a shorter holiday shopping window.

data.title: Kaynes Tech acquires Austria's Sensonic to ramp up railway infrastructure using AI
data.description:
Ramesh Kunhikannan, Managing Director of Kaynes Technology said the acquisition will advance the company's plans to deliver railway safety and efficiency on a global scale.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Kaynes Tech has acquired an Austria-based company Sensonic, in an effort to enhance its capabilities in railway safety and infrastructure management, the company said on December 3.

The acquisition of a controlling stake of 54% has been executed through a wholly-owned subsidiary Kaynes Holding, and it expands Kaynes' presence in global infrastructure technology markets, said the company. The partnership integrates Sensonic’s fiber optic sensing technologies with Kaynes' track record in manufacturing and IoT innovation.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Ramesh Kunhikannan, Managing Director of Kaynes Technology said the acquisition will advance the company's plans to deliver railway safety and efficiency on a global scale.

Sensonic provides what is known as Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS), a solution to monitor railway infrastructure that combines fiber optic sensing with Artificial Intelligence and machine learning. The Austrian company's services turn optical fibers into vibration sensors, which in turn gather critical infrastructure information along their length.

"A multitude of insights are revealed by listening to the railway, including monitoring of track condition, alerting of landslides and rockfalls, identifying security threats and more," the Kaynes statement said about the acquired company.

Kaynes Tech has a market capitalisation of close to Rs 40,000 crore, and the shares are higher by 135% so far this year.

Recently, Kaynes Technology had acquired smart meter company Iskraemeco India for Rs 43 crore, to boost products and services in energy management.

The company is betting of government's plans on the infrastructure front, and says it is hopeful of more traction on railways in the second half. Kaynes sees a potential business of Rs 15,000-20,000 crore from the Kavach System, as an addressable market and has been working to seek approvals.

Rail Vikas Nigam (RVNL) share price will remain in focus on December 4 after the company received a project worth Rs 186.76 crore from East Central Railway.

".... has received Letter of Acceptance (LoA) from East Central Railway for design, supply, erection, testing & commissioning of traction sub stations with associated switching posts for upgradation work of electric traction system from 1X25 to 2 X 25 KV AT feeding system in Gomoh - Patratu Section of Dhanbad Division of East Central Railway,” company said in its release.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The said project is to be completed in 540 days.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

In the month of November, the company emerged as the lowest bidder (L1) from PSPCL for development of distribution infrastructure work for Package-3 Central Zone for execution of HT/LT Infrastructure loss reduction works under reforms-based and results-linked, revamped distribution sector scheme (RDSS) in the state of Punjab. The cost of the work is Rs 642.56 crore and to be completed in 24 months.

Also, secured a letter of acceptance (LoA) from Eastern Railway for an infrastructure project worth Rs 837.67 crore. The project involves earthwork, bridge construction, and railway track laying across 55.2 kilometers between Kalipahari and Pradhankhuta.

The company had reported 27 percent drop in its net profit at Rs 287 crore in Q2FY25. Its revenue registered a marginal decline, reaching Rs 4,855 crore.

Over the past 12 months, the stock jumped more than 150 percent, while Nifty rose 20 percent during the same period

data.title: Daily Voice: RBI to maintain hawkish stance, over 50 bps interest rate cut unlikely in 2025, says Green Portfolio's Divam Sharma
data.description:
Divam Sharma is the Founder and Fund Manager at Green Portfolio

Divam Sharma of Green Portfolio expects the Reserve Bank of India to maintain its hawkish stance. More than 50 bps rate cuts in 2025 does not seem likely, he said in an interview to Moneycontrol.

Furthermore, on Friday, he sees RBI cutting full year growth forecasts because of a plunge in other domestic activities in the remaining quarters.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Although Indian markets present a wonderful long term investing opportunity for the FIIs, the founder and fund manager at Green Portfolio believes that FIIs would not come back in a hurry despite the recent correction in valuations. In fact, "FIIs could take few more quarters before they come back to Indian equities," said Divam with more than 15 years of experience in investment management.

Do you think that even if the RBI starts a rate cut cycle in the coming policy meetings, it may be restricted to a total of 50 bps by 2025?

Yes, we believe that RBI will be following a wait and watch and a gradual process to cut rates. We are yet to see the new regime in the US and macro environment has been increasingly uncertain over the last few years. More than 50 bps rate cuts in 2025 does not seem likely.

Taking a domestic perspective, retail inflation has yet not subsided and GDP is expected to grow at a moderate rate. India's economic growth slowed to near two-year low of 5.4% in the September quarter of this fiscal due to poor performance of manufacturing and mining sectors, but the country continued to remain the fastest-growing large economy. Hence, we expect RBI to maintain its hawkish stance.

Do you see the RBI lowering its full-year growth forecast on Friday?

There could be moderate tapering of growth expectations going forward. As stated before, September quarter recorded a two- year low GDP growth rate. Although the festive season data suggests the revival of consumer activity, the upcoming wedding season would also be a cherry on top, but we still see RBI cutting full year growth forecasts because of a plunge in other domestic activities in the remaining quarters.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Do you see the market recording around 15 percent gains in 2025? If yes, which sectors will be at the forefront?

While it will still be a 50 percent chance of markets achieving a 15 percent in 2025, we believe that selectively there will be some good returns coming in from sectors like sustainability, pharma, and electronics.

The recent correction has thrown in some good opportunities, and we believe that staying invested is the key rather than trying to time the markets. Keeping a valuation cushion is very important as markets can be volatile in 2025.

What are the risk factors for the market in 2025?

While the markets have done great since March 2023, we expect that 2025 would see a flat to mid level returns. Although, economy as a whole did well in last two years, but the rally was also fueled by FOMO, creating a valuation froth, this is pretty evident from the SIPs mutual fund houses were registering. A little plunge in the market was often seen as an opportunity and money was put into wherever deemed possible. But now, markets are staying vigilant. A lot of macroeconomic factors are coming into play, and global economic situation remains uncertain. Power shift in USA, War in the Middle East, China’s trade war remain a few.

What could be the trigger point for FIIs to return to Indian equities?

FIIs would await the clarity coming from the transition of power in the US. Policies like de-dolarization, trade policies with China and other EM’s, policies and ceasefire of ongoing wars, and action towards cutting sovereign debt.

Although Indian markets present a wonderful long term investing opportunity for the FIIs, we believe that they would not come back in a hurry despite the recent correction in valuations.

We believe that FIIs could take few more quarters before they come back to Indian equities.

Is it the right time to focus on the consumption sector?

Consumption sector is facing its own challenges and we believe that the sector will take time beyond 2025 to revive. Consumer discretionary stocks will be on the backfoot as unsecured lending by banks are being curtailed.

Do you expect a pick-up in government capex now?

We believe that capex announcements should come primarily around sustainable infrastructure over the coming months as there is good traction in this domain. Considering that interest rates are high, and that there are uncertainties around global macros, we do not expect much movement from the Government around capex in 2025.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Trading Plan: Can Nifty reclaim 24,550, Bank Nifty hit 53,000 amid likely consolidation?
data.description:
Nifty Trading Plan

The Nifty 50, as well as the Bank Nifty, remained in a strong position on December 3, following a positive trend in global peers. The index formed a bullish candlestick pattern on the daily charts with higher highs and higher lows, while the momentum indicator RSI (Relative Strength Index) showed an upward bias. Hence, the Nifty 50 is expected to hit 24,550 (November high), followed by 24,700 (50-week EMA), which will be the crucial resistance. In case of profit booking, 24,360 is likely to be immediate support, followed by 24,000 as the crucial support, experts said. The Bank Nifty seems to be inching towards the 53,000 mark if it holds 52,600 on a closing basis, while the support lies at 52,200-52,100.

On Tuesday, December 3, the Nifty 50 finished at 24,457, up 181 points, while the Bank Nifty rallied 587 points, or 1.1%, to 52,696, with positive market breadth. About 1,850 shares advanced, against 659 shares that declined on the NSE.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Nifty Outlook and Strategy

Preeti K Chabra, Founder of Trade Delta

Nifty at 24,457 has given a range breakout, and now the earlier resistance of 24,368 (61.8% retracement level of the pre-election result low on the daily chart) has become a new support for the Nifty. With a higher-high and higher-low formation over the last three trading days, and with RSI and MACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence) in a positive crossover, we see the Nifty now in bullish momentum. The target for Nifty is seen at 24,685 (78.6% retracement level in the weekly chart) in this week's expiry.

Key Resistance: 24,685

Key Support: 24,368

Strategy: Traders are advised to initiate a Bullish Butterfly strategy, which is a hedged position with a risk-reward ratio of 1:3. The strategy involves buying one lot of the 24,450 strike Call at Rs 121.5, selling two lots of the 24,650 strike Call at Rs 41.5, and buying one lot of the 24,850 strike Call at Rs 9. The maximum profit is Rs 3,812 if Nifty closes at 24,650, while the maximum loss is Rs 1,188. The breakeven points are 24,498 and 24,802. The payoff of the strategy is as follows.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Ashish Kyal, CMT, Founder and CEO of Waves Strategy Advisors

Nifty managed to close above the 24,350 levels in the previous trading session, which was an immediate hurdle. This level is now broken, keeping the short-term trend on the upside. The prices formed a low near 23,263 levels in November 2024 and rallied sharply higher despite the Adani scandal event and weak GDP numbers. This indicates relative strength.

On the hourly chart, Nifty is witnessing the formation of an Inverted Head & Shoulders (H&S) pattern breakout. The immediate target as per the pattern can be the Gann level of 24,728 on the upside, unless we see a close below the prior candle low. Aggressive targets on the upside, according to the H&S pattern, are at the 25,500 level. Positive momentum can continue till 24,728 or higher as long as prices protect 24,250 on a closing basis.

Key Resistance: 24,728

Key Support: 24,250

Strategy: Long positions can be created above the 24,480 level with a stop-loss at 24,350, targeting 24,560, followed by 24,650 levels.

Om Mehra, Technical Analyst at Samco Securities

The Nifty showed strong momentum after consolidating and broke the resistance zone of 24,350–24,400, which had been a barrier in its previous attempts. As the index heads towards 24,500, the upside momentum looks stable, with 24,200 acting as strong support. Volatility is likely to remain subdued during intraday movements. A minor pullback to the 24,370–24,400 range would provide an ideal re-entry for higher targets. A breakout above 24,500 could fuel further bullish momentum. However, failure to sustain above 24,300 could lead to selling pressure, potentially pushing the index towards 24,200 and triggering profit booking.

Key Resistance: 24,650, 24,700

Key Support: 24,300, 24,200

Strategy: Traders are advised to use a Bull Call Spread strategy by buying the 24,400 strike Call at Rs 148.5 and selling the 24,700 strike Call at Rs 29.6 of the December 5 expiry. The maximum loss is Rs 2,972, and the maximum profit is Rs 4,528, while the breakeven point is 24,519.

Bank Nifty - Outlook and Positioning

Preeti K Chabra, Founder of Trade Delta

The Bank Nifty closed with a strong bullish candle on Tuesday at 52,695, and is now on the verge of a positive breakout with the MACD and RSI in positive crossover territory. The Bank Nifty's high on Tuesday at 52,780 and last Thursday's high of 52,760 represent major resistance. Once this resistance is broken, we can see the Bank Nifty approaching a target of 53,100 (upper range of Bollinger Bands), followed by 53,350 (previous high on the weekly chart). The immediate support for Bank Nifty is Tuesday's low of 52,216 and Friday's high of 52,170.

Key Resistance: 52,780, 53,100, 53,350

Key Support: 52,216, 52,170

Strategy: Buy Bank Nifty Futures above 52,800 with a stop-loss of 52,700, targeting 53,350.

Ashish Kyal, CMT, Founder and CEO of Waves Strategy Advisors

Currently, the Bank Nifty has closed above the Ichimoku cloud, which keeps the daily bias in favour of the bulls. Additionally, the KST (Know Sure Thing) has given a crossover, and a breach above the zero line can suggest that positive momentum will continue. Furthermore, a decisive break above the 52,780 level is necessary for the uptrend to continue. Overall, the trend for the Bank Nifty has shifted positively. A break above the 52,780 level is needed for positive momentum to continue towards 53,200 or higher.

Key Resistance: 53,380

Key Support: 52,200

Strategy: Long positions can be created above the 52,780 level with a stop-loss of 52,380, targeting 53,200, followed by 53,380 levels.

Om Mehra, Technical Analyst at Samco Securities

The Bank Nifty showed strong momentum after a period of consolidation, breaking the previous resistance at 52,760, which had further weakened it. Sustaining above 52,800 would push the index higher. The bullish crossover of the 20 DMA (Day Moving Average) above the 50 DMA signals a positive outlook. Heavyweights from both private and PSU banks are supporting the momentum. The Nifty Bank is maintaining its position above the 61.8% Fibonacci retracement level at 52,680. Furthermore, increasing open interest at the 52,500, 52,400, and 52,300 Put strikes indicates solid support.

A breakout above 52,800 could fuel further bullish momentum. However, failure to sustain above 52,400 may lead to selling pressure, potentially driving the index towards 52,200–52,000 and triggering profit booking.

Key Resistance: 53,200-53,800

Key Support: 52,500, 52,400

Strategy: Traders are advised to apply the Bull Call Spread strategy, buying the 53,000 strike Call at Rs 699.8, and selling the 53,900 strike Call at Rs 329.2 of the December 24 expiry. The maximum loss is Rs 5,558, and the maximum profit is Rs 7,942, while the breakeven point is 53,371.

ONGC has been allotted 490.62 crore equity shares of Rs 10 each by ONGC Petro Additions (OPaL) via a rights issue. After the said allotment, the shareholding of the company in OPaL has increased by 1.12% to 95.69%, from 94.57% earlier.

RVNL has received a Letter of Acceptance for a project worth Rs 186.76 crore from East Central Railway. The project involves the design, supply, erection, testing, and commissioning of traction substations with associated switching posts for the upgradation work of the electric traction system from 1X25 to 2X25 KV AT feeding system in the Gomoh - Patratu section of the Dhanbad Division of East Central Railway.

Remove Ad

Indian Energy Exchange

The electricity exchange achieved an electricity traded volume of 9,689 MU in November, showing a 15.7% year-on-year increase. Despite an increase in power demand, the market clearing price in the Day Ahead Market (DAM) at Rs 3.30 per unit in November declined 17.3% YoY, and the market clearing price in the Real-Time Market (RTM) at Rs 3.46 per unit dropped 13.4% YoY. The DAM volume increased by 9.8% YoY to 5,651 MU, and the RTM volume jumped 28% YoY to 3,019 MU in November.

EPACK Durable

The senior intelligence officer and inspectors of the Special Intelligence and Investigation Branch (SIIB) conducted a search at the company's manufacturing facility in Bhiwadi, Rajasthan on December 2. After a thorough examination of the manufacturing plant, the SIIB officials concluded their visit on the same day without seizing any documents or items.

Nitiraj Engineers

The company has been authorized as a Drone Remote Pilot Training Organization (RPTO) by the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA), Government of India.

Kore Digital

The board has approved the issue of two bonus equity shares of Rs 10 each for every one (1) equity share held as of the record date.

Kaynes Technology India

The company's subsidiary, Kaynes Singapore, has acquired a 54% stake in Austria-based Sensonic Austria. They will deliver technological solutions to customers worldwide.

Hero MotoCorp

The company has extended its title sponsorship of the Hero World Challenge through 2030. Tiger Woods will continue as the global corporate partner of Hero MotoCorp.

Reliance Power

The Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI) has withdrawn the debarment notice issued to the company with immediate effect. Accordingly, the company and its subsidiaries, except Reliance NU BESS, are eligible to participate in all tenders issued by SECI.

PTC India

The Board has appointed Mini Ipe as an Additional Director of the company and designated her as an Independent Director for three years, effective from December 7, subject to the approval of the shareholders of the company. Mini Ipe is a former MD of Life Insurance Corporation of India.

HG Infra Engineering

The company has incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary, HG Banaskantha Bess, on December 3, for the battery energy storage system.

Thyrocare Technologies

The company has made another tranche of equity investment of USD 1 lakh (equivalent to Rs 84,90,000) in Thyrocare Laboratories (Tanzania), the 50:50 joint venture company formed in Tanzania to provide diagnostic and healthcare services.

Wonderla Holidays

The company has opened its Qualified Institutions Placement (QIP) issue on December 3. The floor price has been fixed at Rs 829.74 per share.

Max Healthcare Institute

The company has issued a corporate guarantee of Rs 1,000 crore to Axis Bank for securing a long-term loan to Jaypee Healthcare, a wholly-owned subsidiary of the company.

Bulk Deals

Deepak Nitrite

Motilal Oswal Midcap 30 Fund sold a 0.55% stake in the company at an average price of Rs 2,653.22 per share, valued at Rs 201.6 crore.

Indegene

CA Dawn Investments offloaded a 4.3% stake in the company at an average price of Rs 619.82 per share, amounting to Rs 636.2 crore.

Medi Assist Healthcare Services

Smallcap World Fund Inc offloaded a 2.2% stake in Medi Assist at an average price of Rs 588 per share, valued at Rs 92.6 crore.

Responsive Industries

Foreign investor Brenzett sold a 0.93% stake in the company at an average price of Rs 254.59 per share, amounting to Rs 63.64 crore.

TBO Tek

ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund, SBI Mutual Fund, and AXIS Mutual Fund acquired a 2.56% stake in the company at an average price of Rs 1,465 per share, valued at Rs 408.8 crore. However, Augusta TBO (Singapore) Pte and TBO Korea Holdings sold a 5.87% stake in the company at around the same price, amounting to Rs 934.7 crore.

Zen Technologies

Promoters Kishore Dutt Atluri and Ashok Atluri sold a 2.2% stake worth Rs 360.1 crore in Zen, while Motilal Oswal Mutual Fund bought a 1.23% stake in Zen at an average price of Rs 1,800 per share, valued at Rs 200 crore.

Block Deals

Arihant Foundations & Housing

CaratLine founders Mithun Padam Sacheti and Siddhartha Sacheti bought a 3.49% stake in Arihant Foundations at an average price of Rs 789.15 per share from Monet Securities.

SME Listings on December 4

Abha Power and Steel, Apex Ecotech
